qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stack overflow via deeply nested JSON schema in SchemaParser::Parse
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets compile_json_schema() and SchemaParser::Parse in json_schema_converter.cc, which is a different parser than the BNF grammar parser used by compile_grammar() described in the advisory. The advisory's root cause is uncontrolled recursion in the BNF grammar parser when processing deeply nested parentheses in grammar rules (root ::= ((((...a...))))). While the vulnerability class (unbounded recursion leading to stack overflow) is similar, the vulnerable code path, entry point, and affected component are all different.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded resource consumption via large regex repetition in FSM builder
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets compile_regex() and RegexIR::visit(Repeat) in fsm_builder.cc, which is a different component and mechanism than the advisory's BNF grammar parser. The advisory describes stack overflow from deeply nested parentheses in grammar rules processed by compile_grammar(), while this finding describes memory exhaustion from large repetition quantifiers in regex patterns processed by compile_regex(). The vulnerable code, entry point, exploitation mechanism, and affected component are all different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory (GHSA-7rgv-gqhr-fxg3) describes uncontrolled recursion in xgrammar's BNF grammar parser when compile_grammar() processes grammar rules with deeply nested parentheses (30,000 layers), causing stack overflow (CWE-674). Finding json_schema_deep_nesting_stack_overflow targets a different parser (compile_json_schema/SchemaParser::Parse in json_schema_converter.cc). Finding regex_large_repetition_resource_exhaustion targets a different component (compile_regex/RegexIR::visit(Repeat) in fsm_builder.cc) with a different mechanism (memory exhaustion via FSM state explosion, not stack overflow). Both findings are in different code paths from the advisory's BNF grammar parser vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory PoC uses compiler.compile_grammar() with root ::= followed by 30,000 nested parentheses, targeting the BNF grammar parser.
json_schema_deep_nesting_stack_overflow uses Grammar.from_json_schema() targeting SchemaParser::Parse in json_schema_converter.cc — different entry point and parser.
regex_large_repetition_resource_exhaustion uses Grammar.from_regex() targeting RegexIR::visit(Repeat) in fsm_builder.cc — different entry point, component, and mechanism.
